subject: Cloud Computing
title: Insider Threats in Cloud Environments
description: One-minute skill: Insider Threats in Cloud Environments.

hook.text: A disgruntled employee with admin access deletes the production database before quitting. It happens.

buildup.text: Insider threats come from employees, contractors, or partners with legitimate access who misuse it.

discovery.text: Cloud makes this harder to detect. Actions look legitimate because the identity is authorized.

twist.text: Most insider incidents are unintentional — accidental deletions or misconfigurations, not sabotage.

climax.text: User behavior analytics, audit logs, and separation of duties are the best defenses against insider threats.

punchline.text: The biggest threat might already have a login. Watch the logs.

quiz.question: Why are insider threats difficult to detect in the cloud?
options: The actions appear legitimate because the user is authorized to perform them
Cloud providers do not log any user actions
Insiders always use encrypted connections that cannot be monitored
